DEFINITIVE HARBINGER OF "WOKENESS"Publ. 3/10/2015This is not a novel to be tossed aside lightly....It should be thrown with great force.Dorothy ParkerManipulatingReaders tonthDegree ofMisery and SorrowI hated this. I cannot recall another that so raised my ire. An über leftist pre-meditated kumba-yah-waaah, plodding so far into the wimper-sphere that it could legitimately be considered an ironic harbinger of the kind of paternalistic elitism, against which middle America has arisen in vengeance, TWICE. And you still do not get it! For you those of you who look down your noses in disgust at Walmart shoppers, you sure are a bunch of DUMBASSES!In this [Vulture piece] the author discussed how she was inspired to create a novel of ombré cloth. If you don't know, an ombré cloth is one that by the time you get to the end ispitch black. As Goodreads member Ashley says in her review, "Yeah, let's make art that will stain our souls!!!"If I was writing this review as some sort of literary quasi-critic, I might give the book 3 stars for capable writing and full development of characters, as it thoroughly explores the devastating, lifelong impact of child abuse on the abused. According to Picasso said, "art is the lie that enables us to realize the truth." Unfortunately, the author's intentions were less than admirable in writingA Little Life.Whoever is looking for a book for enjoyment or edification: the ordeal in reading this was way more than I could bear. It is the saddest and gloomiest I've ever read. A doctor who's read this book would have to strongly advise a patient suffering clinical depression to avoid the misery of reading it... at all costs.Understandably then--I think--when I found out that Ms. Yanagihara meant to toy with the emotions of everyone who read this, that she set out to write it in a way that would depress, oppress and dishearten unsuspecting readers, I was pissed. Would I go view a film if I knew the screenwriter's purpose was to inflict gratuitous pain and anguish? NO. This is different than emotions like cheer, fear, and sneers at irony. I know a horror movie's purpose is to scare, The 40-Year-Old Virgin and Apatow rom-coms are intended to cheer me, and movies like Gladiator and Braveheart to evoke and satiate my inner urge for justice and vengeance. I could think of novels if I had more time.Understand me when I say that I have nothing against books that are sad, if such sadness is organic to the story, Lord knows I've read enough of them and they make me upset sometimes. Many I've rated 5 stars. What I am referencing isintentional manipulation of emotions to compose the story tomanufacture pain. I like how Philip Roth explained the difference: “Everybody else is working to change, persuade, tempt and control them. The best readers come to fiction to be free of all that noise.Such deliberate machinations violate the implicit compact between readers and authors: for the former to keep an open mind and suspend disbelief in reading the novel, and for the latter to write a story as truthfully as possible, which means not deceptively attempting to manipulate the reader's emotions. To write the story with its fictive facts in a way that is honest, veridical, organic. Sure, the writer can sway with subtext and mood and motifs. Yet, I don't recall a writer, as did this one, who admits that her purpose in writing this book was to make the reader feel as awful and as sad as possible.Faulkner, no writer of uplifting books himself, said "the writer's privilege" is "to help man endure by lifting his heart." To be sure, I've read plenty of sad novels (including some of my favorites); most of the great ones are, in one way or another, depressing. And yet, when I look back I can find something spiritually uplifting in each.Nothingin this novel lifted my heart.For these reasons, I'm giving the novel 1 star. I only wish I had the option of zero.Also see: review from The London Review of Books [Review]:"He wishes he too could forget, that he too could choose never to consider Caleb again. Always, he wonders why and how he has let four months – four months increasingly distant from him – so affect him, so alter his life. But then, he might as well ask – as he often does – why he has let the first 15 years of his life so dictate the past 28."The answer, of course, is that it’s Yanagihara’s design. That’s why it’s good to know that Jude is entirely her concoction, not a figure based on testimony by survivors of child rape, clinical case studies or anything empirical. I found Jude an infuriating object of attention, but resisted blaming the victim.I blame the author.

Where to even begin reviewing this book? I just finished this book today, and I am so emotionally drained it's hard to put my thoughts into words.If I had to describe this book in one word, it would be devastating. This book is heartbreaking and emotional and full of so much trauma. The main character Jude suffers trauma after trauma after trauma, which the author describes in heartbreaking detail.Despite this book's emotional pain, there are moments of hope, beautiful characters, and loving relationships. Maybe the coolest part of this book is that we follow Jude through his entire life. I have never ever read a book before where we get such a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of our main character's life.If you choose to read this book, proceed with extreme caution. There are so many trigger warnings, and it is such an emotional read, but if you feel that you are in the right headspace to read it, it will be a worthwhile read on trauma and friendship.And to Jude: you deserved so much better

DNF @ 50%(Keep in mind 50% of this behemoth is around 370 pages)I give up. I can't do it anymore. I can't pick this book up and continue reading when every page is so painful. I can handle angst and drama-- and enjoy it most of the time! But this was ridiculous.I strongly caution those with chronic illness, mental illness or disability be very wary of this bookBecause it's not angst. It'storture pornat the disabled character's expense.This is the story of three incredibly selfish "friends" who are mostly so pretentious they don't even resemble real people."None of them really wanted to listen to someone else's story anyway, they only wanted to tell their own."^this consistent determination to be the center of attention isnotfriendship. It's selfish and down right narcissistic. It meant these "friends" consistently saw their friend struggling andnever reach out or intervene.Even worse,Jude's 'suffering in silence' is glorified.This deals with extremely sensitive topics (this entire book is a trigger warning) such as rape, chronic pain, self harm, and suicide. It's obvious from the first chapter that Jude is suffering and his mental health continues to deteriorate-- but he never asks for help and his friends never intervene. In fact, those who reach out or 'complain' are seen by the main characters as 'whiny' or 'crazy.'This establishes the extremely harmful narrative that asking for help makes you an annoyance.It's not accurate. It's not how friendship works. There's nothing noble about suffering alone. Friends support each other and if you need helpit's okay to ask for it.Beyond that, it's not even particularly well written. The character's main traits are their selfishness and pretentiousness, and other than that they fall painfully flat.The characters are essentially pawns for these heavy plot devices.Because those heavy topics I mentioned earlier? The sexual assault and chronic illness? That's all they are here--plot devicesso we can watch as things get worse and worse and worse and see characters lose the will to live.There's even aninterviewwhere the authorbrags about doing very little research:"No, I didn’t do any research; Jude came to me fully formed, and writing his sections were always the easiest. He’s a very consistent character — or is meant to be — which is, arguably, part of what dooms him."Also where we see why her characters lack emotional depth (becauseallmen do, silly!)"I am not that interested in abuse really. But what I am interested in as a writer is the long-term effect it has, particularly in men. I think women grow up almost prepared for it in a way...But I do think that men, almost uniformly, no matter their race or cultural affiliations or religion or sexuality, are equipped with a far more limited emotional toolbox.Also:"I hope that the narrative’s momentum and suspense comes from the reader’s growing recognition — and [spoiler's] — that he’s too damaged to ever truly be repaired, and that there’s a single inevitable ending for him."I can't tell you how harmful it is to present this narrative that people can be "too damaged" and (slight spoilers?) that suicide is the only answer. It's not just incredibly misguided it'sactually incredibly damaging.In summary:this is a book where the main character is applauded for suffering quietly. It establishes a narrative where he can't ask for help-- and then sadistically pushes the story to a tragic ending. Because why? Becauseart?Buddy Read withFaye!

"por isso tento ser amável com tudo o que vejo, e em tudo que vejo, eu vejo ele."é genuinamente impossível absorver esse livro logo depois de lê-lo. nem sei quando vou conseguir dizer que to pronta pra falar dele, mas o que posso dizer agora é que sou uma pessoa diferente de quem eu era há doze dias, antes de começar a ler essa história.

edit dec 2024:yeah who was i kidding, this is a one star book and enough time has passed that i'm not afraid to speak my truth. deep dive coming soon on my substack.“but what was happiness but an extravagance, an impossible state to maintain, partly because it was so difficult to articulate?”edit jan 2022:after much thought, i completely removed my rating from a little life on goodreads. i have never done this before. i would like to explain why.disclaimer: this is my own subjective opinion. i always love discussing books, especially ones i feel strongly about, so feel free to leave yours in the comments.i already wrote what i loved about the book in my initial review (just scroll down). there is no doubt that yanagihara is an incredible author. she's able to make beauty out of nothing; she's able to spin her words into gold. a little life is one of the most stunning books i have ever read and will probably ever read.this is why it is not fair to give it one star. however, personally, it is not fair to give it five stars either.i initially rated it five stars, although i wish i’d never read it. the reason for this was very straightforward: this book transcends storytelling. she managed to uniquely encapsulate the fleeting moments of happiness, friendship, and beauty amidst all the suffering. however, the more i think about and research this book, i realize that this is not enough for me.i usually separate the author from the art, but some situations call for looking at the art through the author's paradigm. this is one of those.jude just suffers to suffer. the violence is so gratuitous and shocking that it feels like a level above horror. because of this, what i believe is the original message faded away as the plot progressed. at a point, it is just so repetitive, so senseless and cartoonish, that i began to feel less for jude. instead, ​it began to feel manipulative and exploitive. as much as i hate this phrase, it began to feel like trauma porn.i have read books that have the triggers that this book has (although not a single book has managed to pack all of them into one). i did not classify them as trauma porn. because usually, the suffering surmounts to a more significant objective or meaning. not for this book.i felt helpless and voyeuristic and nauseous after reading this book. because you cannot help jude no matter how much you want to, because yanagihara wrote jude to be the way he is and never recover or be helped. we are not allowed to help him because nobody is.not only did jude start to feel cartoonish later on, but so did the antagonists. all of them were just cookie-cutter villains, there to hurt jude, to add more to his trauma. i understand that she once said that it was supposed to read more like a fantasy, a twisted fairytale. still, for me, she lost the original beauty of her book because the trauma became so mind-numbingly repetitive.i loved the first two parts more than the rest because those were the only parts that allowed us to glimpse into jb and malcolm’s minds. they are two of the four main friends that yanagihara focuses on, but their lives and voices slowly began to drown the more and more she wrote about jude’s suffering, jude’s misery, jude’s torture.the best parts of this novel were when we could read about the four characters’ close bond and friendship: the way they trusted each other, the way they loved each other. she managed to describe this in ways i had never seen in books, but felt for friends and family in real life.if she had written less of jude’s past or cut down on certain events of his life, if she had expanded further like she originally had about jb and malcolm and willem’s individual lives, it would have been a perfect novel.i felt this when i read it, but i did not rate it any lower because i truly believed that the narration/writing was worth all five stars. however, the more i find out about yanagihara and the things she says in interviews, the less i am able to separate her from her art.here are some things i found. you can easily find proof of this with a simple serach.• yanagihara does not believe in trigger warnings. she believes that it is “very dangerous to isolate oneself from information or art or history or news because the subject is painful”. trigger warnings are there so people do not experience severe mood/mind altering trauma when abruptly encountering something they are affected by. and in a little life, there is a plethora of things to be triggered by.• she also does not believe in therapy. this led me to wonder if there is a connection between this fact and that one of the worst villains in the novel is a psychiatrist. i believe there is.• she stated she did not research the psychological background or trauma responses of jude, the main and most traumatized character, at all. she said that “jude came to her fully formed”.a little life is a good book. it is an excellent book, even, with some of the most beautiful writing i’ve ever read. but after thinking about this book for a few months (i read it august of last year), i’ve realized that a lot of the reason why i gave this five stars is because it evoked strong emotions, perhaps the most i’ve ever felt for a book, from me. while some of these emotions came from reading about the friendship and love in the novel, most came from reading about jude’s trauma.i think we often define a good book as good if it makes us feel something. but the thing is that a little life seems engineered and orchestrated to evoke those exact emotions. if a book’s main motivation is to do that, which i truly think it is, does that still make it good?for those who say that the author didn’t write it for anybody (as she defensively says in every interview), i agree that writing is personal. i agree that writing should be for yourself first and foremost. however, one cannot be so reckless when writing subject matter like this. because these are real problems for real people. just because they are fictional characters doesn't make it any less real. writing about child sexual abuse or suicidal thoughts should come with responsibility.and for a person who doesn't believe in trigger warnings or therapy, writing about them is incredibly dangerous and harmful.the ending too. what is her message out there to readers, real, living, breathing people, who are survivors of similar if not the same things as jude? what is the greater meaning?yanagihara said herself that she wanted to write a character who never gets better. there you have it. because the conclusion is this:it is the reader that suffers the most.this book will stay with me forever. it is equal parts horrifying as it is beautiful. i simply cannot give it a rating due to these reasons.yanagihara may be one of the most capable and brilliant authors i’ve read from, but i will also never, ever read another book from her again.so hanya, if you really want to write for yourself, as you defensively say you do in every interview: keep your writing on your laptop.⸻⸻⸻⸻⸻⸻⸻old reviewi need at least five years to come to terms with what i just read (& to stop crying).review to come but nobody give me shit if they see me reading 500 trashy romance books for the near future⸻⸻⸻⸻⸻⸻⸻oh god. oh my god. after finishing this last night, i cried. i didn’t think i’d cry, i specifically toldelleandleahthat i don’t think i would cry (because i never cry reading books—which isn’t a brag but just the way it is—and also because i thought i’d never make it through this book). but i cried, stared at the wall blankly, and then cried again.anyway. i was torn between giving this book a million stars versus one star. it was one or the other, which i know is weird, but it is partly because it is simultaneously the best book and worst book i have ever read. the best book technical/writing wise, and the absolute worst book emotion wise.do not read this book if you are mentally unstable. i’m serious. i know everyone else says this, but check the trigger warnings. do yourself a favor. don’t read this book, please.there is a sort of timelessness to yanagihara’s writing. i think it may largely have to do with the fact that she doesn’t include any historical markers or any important, present real life political/famous figures. instead, the book is so incredibly insular to the four friends and just tojudein general that you could tell me the book started and ended in the same year and i would believe it.but i think that is why we, as readers, feel so close and so overwhelmed with these characters is because apart from the few other friends of the four that make an appearance, they are all we know. which is why i felt like someone was shoving their hand down my throat and pulling out every emotion i had and didn’t know i had all in one go.i can’t say much without spoiling, but this was a life-changing book for me in the worst ways possible. the writing is so so so lush and beautiful and some sentences i feel like i want tattooed on me forever, but it is also sad and disgusting and at times i had to close the book to take a breath.i kept thinking to myself,is this all there is to life? what is life? why is it worth living?because this book made me feel insignificant and significant at the same time, overwhelmed and empty all at once. the reason why i didn’t think i would cry until i did was because this book made me feel so raw and submerged in every single emotion that i didn’t even know what i was feeling.to be honest, i absolutely hated this book. not because it was badly written or not because it is (and i dare anyone to disagree), literal trauma porn. i got through those parts, although with great difficulty. what i just found unfathomable is that this book was made for the sole purpose of being bleak. and it makes me so so angry because it’s not entirely bleak. there are tiny glimpses of friendship and humor and real love, but it’s just overshadowed by the almost impossible amount of trauma and sadness.stripped down to the bone, to outsiders, jude’s adult life consists of everything we are told to want in our adult lives (and which is, to a very large degree, true). he’s an equity partner at a prestigious law firm. he got his degrees at harvard and MIT. he has a beautiful apartment. he has wonderful (adoptive) parents and his best friend is in love with him. these are facts. these are things that are coveted, that everyone strives to have. and jude has them.but everything is meaningless, empty, hollow, next to jude’s unspeakable childhood trauma. his years and years of sexual, physical, emotional abuse and the resulting self deprecation and isolation has made him unable to ever realize that he too, could be loved. it was gut wrenching that i was reading this eight hundred page book, holding my breath as i flipped through pages, not sure what was to come in the future but knowing that it was going to be harrowing.(which i thought was ironic because that’s how every person who cared about jude felt every second of this book).another problem i had was how cartoonish the antagonists/villains (can i even call them that? they’re beyond any word i can describe) were. how cookie cutter, how two dimensional. i know that the author said she basically thought of every awful thing that could happen to a person and wrote it down, but something about the way jude’s childhood was written makes me not want to believe that this could happen to anyone. it’s the same way i feel about some criminal minds episodes.i can only describe the way in which she wrote the characters, especially jude, as reckless. i feel like this book should have never existed. i wish i had never known about this book. i am so angry. so, so angry, that i don’t even know why i am giving it five stars.

Gave it a shot; not for me, although it may work well for others as the prose is quite inviting and the world dense and inhabited. Felt as if it possibly veered a little too far towards voyeurism and far from truth-telling, which, with the subject matter, wasn't an equation that worked for me.

A Little Lifeis a powerful, disturbing novel. It's full of pain, desperation, and a sense of isolating sadness that sucks the reader into some very dark places. It's also the best book I've read in years.Reading the blurb, you'll get the idea that this work is about 4 college friends and their lives, but that's not entirely true. While each of the 4 main characters, and in fact all the characters in the book, are fully realized with extraordinary character development, the book is really about just one man, Jude St. Francis.Jude is a truly broken person; he's been broken by a childhood that is both a series of horrors that are difficult to read about and a testament to what a human being can endure. Jude doesn't come out of his childhood whole and I feel a little broken by having read about his life. I also feel that strange happiness that comes from being emotionally purged in the way that only great books can accomplish.As Alona mentioned in herreview, this is not a romance, but it is a love story. It's a love story about friendship that tries to overcome pain, and the bravery and sacrifice that true friendship and love sometimes require. The romance in this book is a beautiful one, but not in the traditional sense that a reader might expect or want for the characters involved.We all often say that we loved this character or that character in one of the many books that we read, I know I say it often, but the character of Jude St. Francis is something special. I loved Jude more than I've loved any character before; that's probably why he was capable of so thoroughly breaking my heart. I wanted so much for him, I wanted him to be so much and get so much in life. He didn't get all that I wanted for him, but in the end, I was satisfied with where he ended up and it seemed fitting and very real.I hate the term "triggers", but it's appropriate here. I have a few triggers of my own and they were part of this book, but I felt the writing here just brought me into those places that I don't like to go and left me, not upset or feeling traumatized, but more appreciative of my own ability to survive and thrive. I wanted to reach into the book and take Jude's hand and tell him we'd get through it together.This book is definitely not for everyone, but if you're up to it, you'll be thrilled by the writing. There's pain here, and beauty along with it.Big thanks to Alona for the many messages we exchanged while reading this. It sounds silly, but I feel as if the two of us have survived something together.

book.title: Heart Recaptured
book.author: Tillie Cole
book.description: Even salvation can be delivered through the love of the damned…Beauty can be a curse.Faith can be a cage.Only love can set you free.A few long weeks after being reluctantly ripped from the comforting embrace of her sacred prophet’s religious commune—the only life she has ever known—a terrified Delilah is thrust into a world enveloped by evil and swimming in sin.Steadfastly devout in her faith, and retaining the deep belief that her soul is innately tarnished as a branded ‘Cursed woman of Eve’, Delilah is determined to find her way home to her people in The Order and away from the corrupt and damned outlaw motorcycle club—The Hades Hangmen—who hold her at their secluded compound for her protection—a 'protection' she strongly resents.Delilah yearns to return home, convinced that only amongst her own people, and under the holy guidance of the Lord’s revealed prophet, can her Satan-spawned soul be truly saved. Conditioned her entire life to believe she is a witch... a life-long temptress… the devil’s whore... Delilah increasingly resents her beautiful face, her shapely body and her sensuous effect on men. But when a man of the motorcycle club—a deeply sinful yet stunningly beautiful man—is charged with her care, Delilah begins to see that this dangerous and moralless sinner from the 'outside' may offer her something she did not know could truly exist: unconditional love.Kyler ‘Ky’ Willis loves his life: a daily abundance of brotherhood, liquor, the freedom of the open-road and—best of all—his pick of hot women. Raised a biker brat and now VP of the most notorious MC in the States, Ky has no shortage of club sluts warming his bed; a situation he takes full advantage of… until a certain blonde enters his life… a gorgeous pilgrim-blonde he can’t get out of his head… a pilgrim-blonde he and his club recently-rescued from some backward religious cult… and a pilgrim-blonde he’s been ordered to keep the hell away from and his whorish hands off.When yet another in a lengthy line of drunken blunders forces Ky to reluctantly take charge of the pilgrim-blonde’s care, he realizes that there could be more to this woman than just supermodel looks and a stacked set of tits. He begins to see that she could be the woman who could do the impossible—tame his wild ways and capture his reluctant heart.But the unyielding bonds of Lilah’s past are strong, her ‘people’ determined and, with a new Prophet in charge and hell bent on revenge, they are mightily reluctant to let her go...Dark Contemporary Romance.Contains explicit sexual situations, violence, disturbingly sensitive and taboo subjects, offensive language, and very mature topics.Recommended for age 18 years and up*The Hades Hangmen series has been taken down from Amazon and is no longer for sale.

reviews.text: 5 starsI thought I had my favorites list for 2014 all put together, then I read this book, and had to shuffle everything around. Tillie Cole is an amazing writer and she has this ability to make me feel so much when reading. This book had me awake all hours of the night reading until I finished. I sat there and read for five straight hours, my eyes glued to my kindle until I read the last page. I literally could not put it down. I looked like a zombie the next day at work, but it was so worth it. I cried while reading. I cried while going through my highlights for this review (and I had to go through over 50, because there were too many fantastic quotes). It’s safe to say that this book impacted me in the best way and I loved it dearly.We had been forced to exist after being ripped from our home, ripped from all we knew: the commune. The Order. The Lord’s Prophet. We belonged with God’s Chosen Ones.Lilah is ‘sister’ and friend to Mae. She is not taking life on the outside near as well as her sister. She has been ripped away from her home, surrounded by crude and rude bikers. She’s terrified.Then there is Kyler. VP of the Hangmen. Ky is something else. He’s the biggest manwhore in the MC. He’s hilarious, sexy and crass. He’s also the man that his Prez and best friend, Styx assigns to Lilah. He’s supposed to show her around, help her adjust to ‘real life’. Ky and Lilah are so funny together at first. You won’t find two characters as different from one another as they are.His talk was crude, but I had come to realize it was just his way.The more time these two spend together, the more used to one another they become. They actually start to get along and like being together. For the fist time since Lilah’s been away from the Order, she feels safe. Ky is the only one who makes her feel that way.I felt safe with Ky. I knew he was my protector... And even out in this unfamiliar world, and with as little time spent with him as I had had, I instinctively knew he would protect me from danger.As much as Ky doesn’t want to admit it to himself or anyone else, Lilah is slowly changing him. It’s obvious to anyone around them that he cares for her and is protective over her.My favorite part of the book was just watching these two together. Watching them grow into friends and so much more. I loved seeing the changes they were making. They are so connected to one another. Ky has this way of being able to open Lilah up and bringing her out of her shell, something no one else could do. And Lilah brings out a side of Ky that he didn’t even know existed. He is still his crazy self, but he is sweet, gentle and kind with her. The two of them together… it just made me melt. It made my heart sore.Things had changed for me so drastically in regards to this man. My feelings were so strong that I almost could not stand it. He was becoming the center of my world. He was completely changing my life.Lilah and Ky were two of the most beautiful and memorable characters I’ve read in a long time. Lilah is broken, sheltered, terrified of life, but she has this strength and determination that you don’t see very often. I rooted for her. I loved her. Just as much as I loved Ky, I loved her. My Ky. When I read about Ky in book one, I just knew his book would be epic. Naturally, I was right. Ky made me laugh out loud so many times. Watching him with Lilah made my heart melt. And watching the changes he made for her was a beautiful thing. I adored both of these characters so much. I liked Mae and Styx and the first book, but this book and these characters were on an entirely different level for me.Heart Recapturedis an emotional, beautiful, painful, stunning, raw, gritty and powerful read. It’s a book that will make you laugh, make you cry and it will move you. Ky and Lilah’s love story is one that needs to be read. I can’t recommend this enough. Tillie Cole, you amaze me. I am so looking forward to the next book in this series (Flame). I love the crazy ones and I just can’t wait!As you can tell, this is a must read of mine- a favorite of 2014. Don’t miss out on this amazing story and this beautiful couple!“I love you, Li. You’re mine. My woman. My property. My fuckin’ old lady... my fuckin’ forever.”

So because I LOVE this book, and I wrote it, I'm gonna give it a whopping 5 STARS!!!!Ky, I adore, but it's Lilah that broke my heart, made me want to save her, made me want to make her whole...kept me hoping and fighting for her salvation, peace and happiness.I hope you all enjoy it too! I left a little of my heart behind with this novel.If you are a blogger and would like to take part in Heart Recaptured's Book Blog Tours, sign up below:HEART RECAPTUREDhttps://docs.google.com/forms/d/1WT4L...

5 Brainwashed Stars* Tons of Spoilers*First read Dec 2015Reread May 17 2017Reread 3/27/2022“Fuck, bitch. I’ve been dreaming about making you come since the first time I laid eyes on you… I want you under me, on me, wrapped tight around my cock. I want to fuck you hard, hear you scream… lick you until you can’t take it no more…”-Ky“I am scared… I am so scared of everything… of your world… of you… I do not want to be taken against my will again… I am so afraid…”-LilahI..... Wow I .... OMG ... I don't even know..... Dayum! That was some of the most dark, disturbing, and twisted shit I have ever read. FUCK! This book just gutted me. Talk about emotional. This book made me look at the disturbing aspects of humanity at it's worst. Because the fact of the matter is this shit really does happen. Lilah and Ky's story was heartbreaking, gut wrenching, but also beautiful.“I… I don’t understand… what is… rape?” “What is rape?” I asked, now confused as fuck. “It’s when someone forces themselves on you when you say no. When you have no choice. When you don’t want fucking but they do it anyway. Fuck, bitch, you should know the meaning of that word.” Her eyes went huge. “That has never been done to me…” “Yeah, in that cult, it was.” “No. It was not… rape. It was the elders doing as commanded by the prophet and God.” I closed my eyes and shook my head. The bitch had been raped for years, but had no fucking clue. “One day, Lilah, you’ll get what I’m talking about and realize how fucked up that excuse sounds.”-Lilah and Ky“You complimented my food. You… you said thank you to me for preparing your meal.” “Christ, Lilah…” Her hand covered mine on the grass, and our gazes clashed, feeling the fucking electricity that always hummed between us. “You made me feel like an equal, tonight, Ky. Like I was a woman of worth.”-Lilah and KyKy was awesome. I laughed so hard at the fact that he called everyone a bitch. He was crude, and a total man whore, but I loved him. When he fell for Lilah he fell hard. He stopped his whoring ways, and loved her completely. He did everything he could to protect her, teach her, free her, and make her happy. Every time she would get a little courage and take one step forward something would happen and she would take two steps back. It was hard to read. The things that happen to her. God it's one thing after another. Then when she got kidnapped by the cult again. Fuck that shit was hard to read. To read in detail of her being raped over and over and beat, burnt, tortured. I had to stop so many times. Then when Ky saves her and he sees blood and cum running down her legs and loses it......I was Gutted! Then the fallout after she was saved. OMG! They broke poor Lilah. She was so fucking broke. Hell I almost broke just read it. And telling myself she isn't a real person didn't help at all, because all I could think was this shit is happening somewhere in the world right now. Truly some of the hardest shit to read.I didn’t wanna fuck any sluts when she was around. Hell, when she wasn’t! I only wanted one pussy… hers, even if the bitch it belonged to was some kind of other-level crazy. She stirred things in me that I didn’t even know existed, made me think things about property patches and putting my club colors on her back. My eyes snapped open at these thoughts, and I knew one thing. I fuckin’ loved the bitch. I was fuckin’ in love with the bat shit-crazy pilgrim blonde taking a shower in my bathroom right now… naked.-Ky“They fuckin’ beat you, lashed you… THEY FUCKIN’ RAPED MY BITCH! Upgraded to 5 Stars***Book One Spoilers***So I have been looking forward to this thing for what feels likeforever.Now, being that was the case, I was a little bit...nervous.Why you say?Because I seem to have been in a bit of a book slump lately, with several books I have been looking forward to for awhile (like this one) being kinda, sorta, okayverydisappointing.So I kind of went in to this book like this:Now, I loved the first one in this series. Styx and Mae were really awesome. Their back story, his speech issues, and her religious cult background - all against an MC club backdrop made for a very interesting story.The set up for this second book was also done very well; however, I did have one concern going into this one:Ky and his sluts, Tiff and Jules.There were some - ahem, high ick factor threesome scenes in the first book involving these two whores - and I was concerned that Cole would not be able to navigate these treacherous, skank-ridden waters in a manner that wouldn't leave me shipwrecked on Disgusted Island.But thankfuckTille Cole is a total rock star - and a marvelous sea captain - because not only did she manage to steer through those shenanigans in a way that alleviated any issues for me, she totally blew my expectations for a resolution in that arena out of the water.I ended up really loving Ky and Lilah grew on me for sure. Sometimes I wanted to straight up slap her, but I had to remind myself that the poor brainwashed little beauty had literally no concept of the outside world and could only make choices based on the knowledge with which she had been provided up to that point in her life - false though it may be.Something I also really liked about this story is that Cole didn't sugarcoat some of the scenes and events in this book that some writers might have, thus lending the book even more authenticity and intensity, which I always appreciate.I have to say that I am finding that I am having a hard time writing this review without including any spoilers for some reason today, so I will just say this:If you liked the first one, you will definitely like this one. And if, like me, you are going in like this:Rest assured, you will come out like this:And now, myself and Antonio down below have to wait for another forever for book three.Le sigh.XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXOnly 2 more days!!!!XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX6/4/14More Hangmen? Oooo, *orgasmic shudder*What do you think Antonio?Need now, need now...

3.75 StarsWell, this escalated quickly..Definitely darker and sicker than the first book.I definitely did like it, but I can’t help feeling like the love connection betweenLilahandKyhad something missing. There were moments when I totally believed it but then there were some when I rationalised some things, and it just made me doubt the genuineness of it.I understood the need to highlight how beautiful and desirable Lilah was, because that was the source of all her misery.Her beauty was vilified,used to explain her worth for most of her life and generally speaking, for her,it was her downfall.Now, we know it was all a bunch of BS, that those disgusting religious fanatics used in order to justify their pedophilia, and sick as fuck minds.. it made me physically ill to read those passages where she was a young child accused of seducing men, I just can’t .. I was beyond horrified.This is not a book you can easily read, without having an extreme reaction of disgust.Now, the reason I mentioned that, is becauseI wished Ky would have been less entranced with her beauty. The author took the necessary steps to show that he loved her for herself and not just for her beauty but somehow I wished that she would have made that point earlier in the story. I know Ky wasn’t as superficial as his careless words made him seem but I also think that if she wasn’t such a knockout he wouldn’t have been tempted to spend as much time with her, getting to know her which eventually led him to fall in love. I understand that initial physical attraction is how most people get together and explore a relationship but here I didn’t feel like they had anything to build on.I just didn’t feel like they had all that much in common or that they understood each other on a deeper level.Ky was kind of obsessed with her looks and he talked about that extensively until the very end of the book, which I have to admit got very tiresome to read about.Apart from the constant talk of her looks, the one other thing that was suspicious to me was that she healed from a brutal attacked too soon, too easily. I get that we got“that scene”which I suppose helped her settle that nagging voice in her head but I just think that no matter how used someone is to abuse, when something of that high a magnitude happens, you don’t get over it so easily. That’s not to say, that she magically recovered and was all sunshine and rainbows. I suppose it depends how you look at it, I don’t have much information on trauma victim behaviour and I know each is different, therefore highly subjective.Now for the things I did love.I liked the path thatLilah’scharacter growth took. She went from denial, to suspicion, to resignation andeventually she found purpose in the ruins of her crumbling belief.. That to me was a hard but beautiful journey. I don’t believe in instant change of personality, motivation or belief which is why I loved that Tillie build a believable journey of self discovery for her.As forKy, it’s easy to dislike his superficial ways but somehow I found myself liking him. I usually like characters that talk big, are jovial, crass, confident but at the same time they don’t actually take themselves so seriously. It’s more of an imagine which crumbles when you look past the words and focus on his actions instead.So yes, I liked him.Overall, this book was harder to digest than the first one. It’s hard to enjoy an HEA when you know the road that lead to it was peppered with so much pain, abuse, destruction and shattered innocence. But,I also believe that it’s essential to show that healing can occur even in the most broken parts or our minds/bodies.Onto Flame and Maddie now. I just know they’ll break my heart..

5 Heartbreaking STARS......(If I could,I would give it more than 5 stars...this book was perfection!!!)Such an amazing book!!I don't know how to start my review for this book.Maybe because I'm still upset and heartbroken,sad, happy...So many feelings!!!I liked the first book but with this one I fell in love!!!From the first page I felt I was part of the story!!The story is captivating,brilliant and unique!!!Be prepared because there are so many crazy people here!!!There were moments I was pissed,other times I was happy and sometimes I cried a lot..Especially in the last chapters I couldn't stop...I was so shocked...I wanted to be there and hug Lilah...Definitely one of the best books I have ever read!Ky is Styx's best friend and VP of the Hades Hangmen.He loves women a lot...You can say he is a man whore!!Lilah is one of Mae’s sisters.All these years she thought she was cursed.According to her religion ,because she was so beautiful they told her she was evil.When the Hades Hangmen saved her and her sister Maddie from the commune she had lived , Lilah struggles to become a part in this world while she still has her beliefs.Styx will put Ky shows Lilah how they live...And while Ky starts to spent time with Lilah,he will want her for himself....I can say officially say that I'm in love with Ky!!!! He is smart,sexy,kind,protective and sweet around Lilah!!And funny!!!OMG he makes me laugh so many times with his smart answers!!!!What makes me love him more was the love he saws in Lilah..the way he tried to make her understand that she isn't evil.The love that Ky has for Lilah is so beautiful.!!OMG Poor Lilah...In her whole life she was abused and tortured.I don't think I have ever felt so much sympathy for a character before!!I think she is the most tortured heroine...She is so pure and sweet and I can understand what Ky finds in her!!After this book,I can read whatever Tillie Cole writes!!! She has proved that she is an amazing author and she managed to surprise me with her brilliant story!! I highly recommend it to everyone who wants a different and unique story!!But be prepared because it will break your hearts...You will need Kleenex!!!
Fall/Winter2014? ;_;

*****4.5 STARS*****{Buddy Read with my Boo}"Before you I was vacant of love. Now I am replete. Before you I lived without hope. Now I am inspired. Before you I was broken. Now I am whole."I feel like I just rode a wild roller coaster of emotions. This book was just absolutely amazing!! This is most definitely my favorite series by Tillie Cole. I have read other MC books but these are so unique. So different. The characters are incredible. In the first book, I was very much intrigued by Ky, the VP of theHades Hangmen. The man is described as strikingly handsome and a manwhore. I knew he was going to be rethinking his bad boy ways once he set his sights on Lilah, one of the beautiful women labeled"the Cursed", by the sadistic Cult they belonged to.We pick up from the end of the previous book, where Mae's fellow "Cursed" sisters are rescued from the Cult compound. They are now residing at the Hangmen's club and are having a difficult time acclimating to their new surroundings. Lilah seems to be having the hardest time adjusting. She has been so thoroughly brainwashed by the evil cult leaders. She actually hopes to be sent back to them. It was hard trying to understand how she could want to go back to the people who molested her as a child and raped her repeatedly for most of her life. She and her fellow "cursed" sisters were told they were the spawn of Satan. They were treated as less than human.Ky is captivated by Lilah. At first he makes inappropriate advances toward her which freaks her out and lands him in hot water with Styx. He is forced to become her teacher in the ways of regular society. He must get her to accept her new life style. Little by little he is breaking through to her. Oh how I started to fall for this man. He was rough around the edges but he tried with everything he had to empower this broken woman. He taught her to speak up for herself. To walk beside him and not behind him as if she were beneath him. He wanted her to feel as if she were his equal.As time goes on, these two fall in love. I was so happy but at the same time a feeling of dread was always in the pit of my stomach. The cult was still very much alive and plotting their vengeance against the Hangmen. Also Lilah was still holding on so very strongly to the messed up beliefs that she was brainwashed with. She was taught that her beauty was evil. That they seduced men to do Satan's bidding. She believed no one could love her for who she was. Men were only drawn to her for her physical beauty.Towards the 80% mark, some major angst and pure gut wrenching torture occurs. I seriously had to break from the book. It was to me, too much, and my Bestie agreed. We felt some of what happened was just going a tad bit overboard. Just when I thought everything was going to be okay, something else happens. I was swearing that I was going to rate the book a two at this point. My heart just couldn't take one more horrible thing happening. Honestly, I was yellingSTOP IT!!!!Well I love where the author ended up taking the story. What could have been horrible, actually turns out making this love story just tremendously beautiful to me."Then it was all worth it.""What was, Li.? What was all worth it?""Everything...Every single second of my life...because it eventually led me to you. It led me to fall so deeply in love with you, Ky...the man who recaptured this bruised and battered heart."I can't wait for Flame and Maddie's book next! I have a feeling this one is going to be even more heart breaking. For those of you who love MC books as much as I do, I really do recommend this series. You're in for a crazy ride!!

Livehttp://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/B00QZLS...5In love with KyStarsHeart Recapturedbegins where things left off inIt Ain’t Me Babe. The club has invaded the commune and have taking the Maddie and Lilah with them."Nope. You’re coming with us, sweet cheeks, so stop trying to run this sexy ass away. Although, I could watch that fuckin perfect peach of a view all day long and never get tired of it. But Mae wants you at the club, so you’re coming to the club.”The girls are completely at a loss for what to do now. The difference in Mae inIt Ain’t Me Babeis that she ran away, she knew the commune was a cult, but Maddie and Lilah were taken and still struggle with what they have been taught their whole lives. They have been brainwashed for years and is hard for them to see how wrong things were at the commune. Then throw in the fact that they went from a commune to a motorcycle club, one extreme to the next, it is hard for them to adjust.After choosing to stay locked away in the motorcycle club, Styx enlist Ky to help Lilah adapt. She has been watching Ky and he has been watching her. Lilah feels he is entranced with her looks and truthfully I would say Ky is, but the difference is Lilah was taught that her looks put men under a spell and she was the devil’s tool to tempt men. Despite leaving the commune she can’t be reprogrammed overnight, it is a process to teach her and let her see the world for real."Lesson two, there is more to life than being caged in a protective bubble. You had it at that cult; now you’re locking yourself away here. Gotta take life by the balls at some point and squeeze those fuckers with the skill of a whore.”I love Ky and his attitude toward life. He has such a great personality and despite his gruff exterior he wants to help Lilah. Throughout their little expeditions and time spent together they develop a friendship. Their friendship leads to more as Ky begins developing feelings for Lilah. Now, Ky isn’t some expert on love, he was raised by a man who taught him to use woman.He has lived by this motto his whole live until he meets Lilah, she is his game changer. She is innocent, sweet, and sees the best in him - the man he could be. Lilah has never known love, at a young age she was taken from her family and held as a prisoner for “tempting men.”The connection between Lilah and Ky is beautiful and while reading it I could feel their love. Their connection was so sweet and there were moments my heart broke for them. There were also moments of intense heat.Based on my review up until this point you are thinking this is a sweet love story, but there is so much more to this story. This is a legit MC book where there is real club drama. I love that the drama is original and not something we read about in tons of other MC books. The MC drama continues fromIt Ain’t Me Babeand only intensifies. The last 30% of this book held me completely captivated. It was intense, heartbreaking and had me on the edge of my seat.Tillie Cole is an amazing writer! How she can go from writing a book about an Alabama football player, to a Motorcycle Club in the midst of fight with a cult, and both be so good is beyond me. I absolutely love her writing and think she is extremely talented.I highly recommend this series!ARC kindly provided in exchange for an honest review.

4 Recaptured Stars.In the last book we saw how Kyle was immediately taken by Lilah after their rescue from the cult. But she’s too much of a religious nut-case and he’s too much of a manwhore and badass biker to ever act on it. When Styx tells him that he have to help Lilah adjusting to the outside world everything changes for them and what begins with a strange friendship soon turns into something more.But when two different worlds collide and Lilah’s past keeps haunting her, how does two people with opposite views of life find happiness together?What I love about these books is how Tillie Cole put together two different realities (MC world and Religious Cults) and created a love story. And not only she created a beautiful love story but she also did it brilliantly. This series are a little heavy, not only for the Biker’s world aspect, because when you read a book with bikers you know what to expect, but mainly because all the religious features and the blind faith these people have. It’s disturbing to see how brainwashed these people are, and how distorted their view of the world and of themselves is. Tillie Cole described that perfectly, so you should not expect a cupcake-pink romance. This is gritty, emotional and will test your patient and comfort zone limits. It’s one of those readings that will leave with a huge hangover, but it’s too good to pass. If you are not easily impressed, these books are a must read.One thing I know for sure is that I don’t care how epic Rider’s book will be: There’s absolutely no way I’m reading it. After the first book I would probably give him a try. After this one? His book will straight to Nopeland. I refuse to read a romance book about a guy that accepts rape trough passiveness. No. Moving On.Rating:4 Stars.Characters Development:I had a little trouble with Ky in the beginning, due to his “manwhoreness”, but once he admitted his feelings he was impossible not to love him. Under all that beard, tattoos and bad-boy look he was sweet, selfless and I love how he fought for Lilah. And Lilah… damn, that was hard for me!! Sometimes I would have to stop reading, deep breathing and mentally repeating several times: “She was brainwashed by a cult. It isn’t her fault!!”. Her blind faith tested my limits at many moments but eventually she grew on me… very near the end. It was nice to see more of the previous characters and I love how Flame is protective of Maggie, I can’t wait to read their book!!Steam:Hot.Sensible Subjects:Rape. Mental and physical abuse. Violence.Love Triangle:No.Cheating:Technically, no. But if you don’t like books where it’s obvious the hero cares for the girl but keeps hoe-ing around, you will not like the beginning of this book.HEA:Yes.

4-4.25 StarsAn emotional, raw, romance about two very different people falling in love."Before you I was vacant of love. Now I am replete. Before you I lived without hope. Now I am inspired. He lives his life the way he wants and makes no excuses, embracing the motto his father taught him.Pussies are to be licked good and fucked hard - never worshipped."Delilah is a devout follower of The Order. As a 'cursed woman of Eve' she has spent her life with the hope that if she follows the teachings of the Prophet, she will be able to save her soul. With her commune gone, all Delilah wants to do is find a way back to her people, and free herself from the evil she is convinced is within her.I stared at the girl reflected in the glass, my lip curled in disgust. She was beautiful; flawless golden skin, long blonde hair, cornflower blue eyes...a most impressive disguise. The creation of utmost evil.When Ky finds himself responsible for the 'pilgrim bitch' he begins to see that there is more to Lilah than her stunning good looks. Ky finds himself falling for the beautiful, but very broken Lilah. While she struggles to understand the outside world, and her feelings for Ky. Will he be able to break the hold her past has on her? Can these two find love in the darkest of circumstances?Heart Recapturedwas not an easy read, it contains a lot of disturbing themes and situations. But, I found myself invested in Ky and Lilah, and their story, while not perfect, was very sweet in the end.“Every single second of my life...because it eventually led me to you...it lead me to fall so deeply in love with you, Ky...the man who recaptured this bruised and battered heart.”

I do not think we will be safe here. In fact, I think Mae has dropped us into the depths of Hell."Ky is a manwhore, no other way to describe him but this manwhore has met his match in Lilah. Even though everyone else can see that Lilah was rescuedawayfrom very evil people she still believes in everything she'd been taught growing up, so unlike every other female he encounters Lilah doesn't throw herself at Ky's feet.I was not attracted to Ky in any way for a few reasons:1) I am not attracted to blondes (most of the time).2) He was way to conceited, it's good to have confidence but Ky took it to the next level.This would in most cases kill a book for me, come on who wants to read a book and not like the hero?So I was totally surprised that it didn't bother me at all. The characters were all so diverse and well written and the fact you get so many different POV's throughout didn't give me a chance to get bored.Mae, Lilah's 'sister' is really worried about her adjusting to the outside world like she has so she asks her man Stynx the president of the Hades Hangmen MC to help. Stynx tells Ky his VP he has to watch and protect Lilah, so even though it is obvious what will eventually happen the story to get there was great. I can't believe I am going to say this but I even found myself interested in the chapters about the cult in New Zion and I used to despise anything about religious sects.So as a person with no religious experience I did find some of the religious talk a bit much and eye roll worthy at times however it was interesting and really needed to tell Lilah's story. As Lilah had been brainwashed by it her whole life, being told her amazing beauty was a curse to lure men to the devil and she struggles with what her beliefs are through the entire book.Ky did not truly love me. He was under my spell. He said so himself, from his very own lips. It was an illusion. I had ruined my purity, my virtue, for a man entrapped by my allurement.This 'cursed' label is driving Lilah crazy until she comes up with a plan.I am really excited to read Flame & Maddie's story next, I've heard great things and Flame's dark and twisted just the way I like 'em.

***4.5 Stars***THIS. BOOK. TORE. ME. UP.My heart broke and bled for Lilah! She was so confused, so distraught, so brainwashed, so damaged and tortured...she questioned everything, understood nothing, felt so deeply!Lilah was ripped away from The Order, the only life she's ever really known. One of the Cursed, she was a devout follower, a true believer...of all the girls, the one most rooted in the teachings.Ky is tasked with protecting Lilah, showing her the world, teaching her how to live outside of the commune walls. At first she's scared, terrified of all the sin around her...but for some inexplicable reason she feels safe with Ky, and begins to slowly absorb the beauty of the world around her.Little by little, Ky and Lilah forge a deep bond. Ky may be opening up Lilah's eyes to the world around her, but he also allows her to express her faith and religious rituals. Ky is so used to his whoring ways...one of the most notorious sluts in the MC...but he's drawn to her. He doesn't understand it. His father always told him that woman are to be used, never worshipped...and he's lived his life by that principle, but he finds himself very protective of Lilah."You are not that way with me. You are caring. You are loving. You are my teacher, my protector. You keep me safe. Ky, you ARE my safety."Lilah brings out a side of him he didn't know he possessed. He finds himself opening up parts of himself that only his best friend Styx knows about....she didn't know who she was without her faith...funny, 'cause I didn't know who the fuck I was without her.Simply, Ky has fallen in love with Lilah. He doesn't know how it happened, or why it happened, but it did. And he intends to fully make her his."I'd kill for you, baby. Anyone even comes close to trying to take you away from me and I'll slit their motherfuckin' throat. You belong to me, you're mine, and right now, in this bed, I'm gonna do something else for the very first time. We BOTH are.....I'm gonna make love to you, Lilah. I'm gonna take you as mine, possess you. 'Cause there ain't no one out there...no one else who could do this to me, but you."However, no matter how deeply Lilah returns his feelings, she knows in her heart that his love is a ruse. He is simply entranced by her beauty, another fallen man caused to sin from the temptation of her looks, her body. She has the devil coursing through her veins, and she will never be rid of her curse.In the midst of all this, The Order is rebuilding. They now have a new leader, and under this new prophet they will become a stronger group, more divine. I will repeat the same thing I said from book one...the things that happen in this commune are difficult to read about. It is vile, it is disgusting....it is unbelievably cringe-worthy. The followers commit these acts to be closer to God, closer to salvation.I will warn you that the last 25% of this book are intensely difficult to read. The things that Lilah has endured are absolutely brutal. Not only does it break her physically, but emotionally, mentally and spiritually. She is torn between her heart, mind and faith. She doesn't know what to believe, what to feel, what to think...the only thing she knows to be true is her love for Ky. Her love is deep...her love is pure.This is an exceptionally beautiful book...difficult to read, difficult to wrap your head around, but unbelievably satisfying in the end. Lilah's journey is painful and heartbreaking, but worth it in the end."You are the embodiment of every divine word that could slip from my lips. I adore you, Ky. I can no longer imagine my life without you in it. You are my white dove. You fill me with peace, love and devotion."Her soul was slowly chipped away by vile and disturbed men, before it finally broke. But Ky was there to help put her back together again...to fill in the missing pieces...to finally give her the peace she yearned and bled for."Before you I was vacant of love. Now I am replete. Before you I lived without hope. Now I am inspired. Before you I was broken. Now I am whole."This is a series that is completely addicting and unbelievably thought provoking. I know some might have a difficult time with the religious aspect of this series, with this book especially heavy with it, but I am completely absorbed by it. It's such a unique and interesting layer to what might have otherwise been another run of the mill MC series.All that's left for me to say is.....GIVE ME FLAME!! I cannot wait for his and Maddie's story!! Definitely one of my most anticipated reads of the year!***Why not 5 stars?? As with the previous book, this book is riddled with editing issues. There are words missing from sentences, extra words repeated and awkward sentence structure. There are just too many to overlook and took away some of my reading enjoyment. When I'm in the middle of an intense scene, I don't want to have to reread a sentence to make sure I understand what's going on.

★★★3.5 Stars★★★Now Live!Heart Recaptured starts off exactly where It Ain't Me Babe finished - Styx, Ky and Mae rescuing her sisters fromThe Order, which is aApocalyptic New Religious Movementthat focuses on 'the Lords Sharing' and the degradation of women. When they free Lilah and Maddie, it's clear that its going to be a long battle to help them integrate into the outside world. Ky is smitten with Lilah's beautiful face and body, which is the very thing that she believes is her curse.Lilahis unlike Mae in that she does not want to live outside of The Order, she hasn't lost her faith and she truly believes that she is cursed, born sinful, and that her beauty is hers solely to lure men into temptation. From a young age she has been abused and hated for her looks.Kyis the VP and best friend toThe Hades HangmenPresident, Styx. He is only interested in one thing other than his club, and that is getting as much pussy as he can. Bought up by his father, an original Hangman, he was told that'pussies are to be licked good and fucked hard - never worshipped'and he has always lived by that motto.Styx tasks Ky with helping Lilah learn about her new surroundings, it's more of a punishment for treating her badly when she first arrives, but Styx would do anything to make Mae happy so he asks Ky to show Lilah what life is like outside the commune and be respectful while doing so. With her long grey dress and white headdress, Lilah is not like the club sluts that he is used to being around, but he soon finds that he enjoys talking with the pilgrim blond with blue eyes and big lips.Lilah is finding it extremely hard being immersed in club life, it is her idea of hell but is confused when she realises that she feels safer than she has ever felt before when she is with Ky. She has been used by men in her commune and so when Ky doesn't take her against her will, she feels protected.Meanwhile, the club is involved in a war against the Ku Klux Klan and Prophet Cain (Rider) is creating a brand new movement in a merged commune - the only problem is that he needs Mae to fulfil his prophecies.What I loved about this book was watching Ky and Lilah change each other, and see their feelings grow and evolve. There were some extremely difficult scenes that broke my heart and some equally beautiful moments that made me smile.My only complaint was that there were far too many religious aspects and quoting from scriptures etc, I am romance greedy and would have loved to have had more of Lilah and Ky together and some more of the MC. I love the Hangmen and after reading this one, I am absolutely gagging for Flame and Maddies' book. He is an extreme nutcase and I can't wait to get into his head.Overall, if you loved IAMB, you probably won't be disappointed and if you love to see a complete man-whore brought to his knees by a sweet and innocent (but slightly spunky) heroine, then this is the book for you. Less of the religious doctrine and I would have rated a solid 4 stars.***ARC provided by author in return for an honest review***

You eat with me, walk with me, not two fuckin’ feet behind, and you never let yourself do otherwise.”“You may not wanna fuck me yet, baby, but you let me touch you, have your mouth on mine and now I ain’t ever gonna stop touching you, kissing you. Yeah?”For the first time in my life, when I was with Ky, I did not feel like Delilah, a Cursed sister of Eve, just plain Lilah...an ordinary girl who had finally been kissed by a boy.“Don’t fuckin’ hide from me. Show me those sweet beautiful blues.”“Psalms are poems; you are mine. You are the embodiment of every divine word that could slip from my lips. I adore you, Ky.”“You are caring. You are loving. You are my teacher, my protector. You keep me safe, Ky, you are my safety.”“It was all worth it. Everything. Every single second of my life...because it eventually led me to you... the man who recaptured this bruised and battered heart.”“I love you, Li. You’re mine. My woman. My property. My fuckin’ old lady... my fuckin’ forever.”Posted: July 6, 2016

DNF at 60%I think me and Tillie Cole are just not meant to be. Everyone else loves her books but my nickname stands true, I am the wildcard reader and they aren't for me.I think the idea of this series is very interesting, cults and mc clubs. I just don't like the characters.

Title:Heart RecapturedSeries:Hades Hangmen #2Author:Tillie ColeRelease Date:December 14, 2014Rating:5 starsCliffhanger:. No.HEA:Yes.“We are all fallen in some way, Lilah.”Heart Recaptured totally blew my mind. I am still a little bit breathless even after hours that have passed since I finished this fabulous roller coaster ride. This is certainly one of the best books Tillie Cole has written so far.It’s a heart throbbing, intense MC story. It’s full of love, passion and intensity. Absolutely stunning and gut-wrenching, gritty, raw and beautiful Heart Recaptured is a book like no other. And it definitely goes into my the best of 2014 list.After reading ‘It ain’t me babe’ few months ago, I was hooked. It was spectacular start of powerful series and I was anticipating the sequel from the moment I finished the last page of the first book. When I finally started Ky and Lilah’s story I knew it would be good, but I certainly didn’t expect it to be that mind blowing! I cannot put in words how much I loved Heart Recaptured. I lived and breathed this story. I felt it in every cell of my body.Delilah was raised in strict commune. She never knew the life outside of the fence; so now that she’s been freed by Hades Hangmen, it doesn’t feel like that for her. She’s missing the old ways. The real life seems for her like hell. Especially with all that hedonistic bikers. They fuck, they drink, and generally are demons…Kyler is The VP of Hades Hangmen; he’s crude, vulgar and loyal to the club. He was also asked to show Lilah how is the real life outside of commune.“Every single second of my life...because it eventually led me to you...it lead me to fall so deeply in love with you, Ky...the man who recaptured this bruised and battered heart.”Heart Recaptured is painfully strong story; it’s about survival and redemption. It’s a brilliant romance between a broken, lost young woman and rough around the edges man. They seemed so different, but yet so perfect for each other. I am so in love with Ky and Lilah! They both made this novel shine.”I felt safe with Ky. I knew he was my protector... And even out in this unfamiliar world, and with as little time spent with him as I had had, I instinctively knew he would protect me from danger.But be aware! This book is real roller coaster. You will be laughing, you will certainly be turned on, and you will need tissues close to you when the time comes.I need another book in this series right this second.“You are my unattainable dream come true.”*Review Copy was provided to me by the author, Tillie Cole, in exchange for honest review *FOLLOW SMOKIN HOT BOOK BLOG ON:

5++++++ Recaptured Hearts StarsI almost couldn't deal...this book waspure torture. For the first time in my life I felt like I needed someone to hold my hand while reading...I needed my mommy! But oh man I LOVED it! Ky, theHades Hangmen Whore*sigh* I'm in love with Ky. Yes I'm Sher and I'm madly in love with a fictional character!This story took me on such an intense, emotional journey likenever before, (well besidesThe Bronze Horseman, that book almost killed me). Ky, what can I say about Ky. He was just a well rounded, solid character. My kind of funny, lovable, asshole hero, but with redeemable qualities, you just have to give him a chance. He worms his way through to your heart and you cannot help but fall for him. The sweetest part is the way he gets redeemed by love. Who would've thought a Jesus freak, "virginal pilgrim" girl would be the one to redeem such a selfish, sinful man?Lilah was brainwashed since birth by her religious cult. Unlike Mae in book 1, she did not willingly leave her place in the sadistic cult. It was all she knew. I love how Ky was so loving and patient with her. He protected her and guided her like a true alpha male should. I really liked Lilah and I do think she's earned a place on my favorite heroine list. Her transformation was such a beautiful one, but most of all, I loved how even to the very end she held on to her faith and I empathized with that. It was painful seeing how tortured she was and what she endured and that alone made me ache in my heart for a happy ending for her. I've never been so happy for an HEA for two fictional like I have been with Ky and Lilah. They sincerely deserved it.I ADORE the secondary characters, besides KA, Tillie Cole is the master at introducing and developing each character until you're dying to hold their story in your hands. Each scene was filled with such deep feels that at times I had to rest my book down and count to ten. After meeting TC and hearing how much research was put into these books I appreciated the storyline even more. Sadistic cults can be intense, some of the teachings can seem absurd and sometimes the effects of brainwashing never fully vanishes and storylines like this can be tough for those who just can't grasp the effects and damages wrought by religious cults. Tillie Cole has proven herself to me as a master storyteller. I say that because the unfolding, the renewing, the transformation of Ky and Lilah was spectacular. It couldn't have been an easy task. But she did it and she did it well.I'm obsessed with this series and I'm so excited to see what will become of secondary characters. This is a very dark read and super intense, but it's worth the read that's for sure.

if i wasn’t so spiteful, i’d have given this book 3 starsi don’t think an author in history has hated a character they created as much as tillie cole hated delilahwhy did she need to spend her whole life being molested and raped to then get kidnapped, tortured, raped, and burned at the stake after being given the tiniest sliver of happiness 💀then the girl had a major psychotic break and shot flame (loml), nearly shot her sister, and permanently disfigured her own facethat being said…i still couldn’t get over how annoying she wasdelilah whenever someone calls her beautiful—i love how delilah had a breakdown and scarred her face to be “ugly” but ky’s inner thoughts are like“she’s even more beautiful now 🥰🥰”BOY-the prophet cain POVs were so unnecessary. like i couldn’t give a less fuck about this psychopaths god complex 🥱 to believe this man is getting his ownbook?! all this man deserves is an early graveflame gets 5 stars for his possessive and barbaric attitude towards maddie 😍💗🫂him stalking her and cutting himself when he thinks she’s been hurt 😌🤌🏻hades hangmen↠ #1it ain’t me, babe—3.25 stars↠ #2 heart recaptured —2.25 stars

FIVE+++ STARS((Photos via Ben Dahlhaus Facebook & Candice Swanepoel Facebook))“You are my unattainable dream come true.”Words that describe this story:Heartbreaking.Beautiful.Tearful.Tragic.Unbelievable.You know, every single time I finish a Tillie Cole novel, I have the hardest time articulating how much I love it. I don’t know how she constantly does it. The depth of information and emotion that went into this story is very apparent. Like “It Ain’t Me, Babe,” this story is unlike any other MC stories out there. This wasn’t an easy read. You will find some scenes tough to stomach and you will be shocked. And be warned: You will cry (but in a good way).Pussies were created to be licked good and fucked hard – never worshipped.Ky is the VP of the Hades Hangmen, and as an outlaw, he will not hesitate to kill those who threaten his club and those he cares about. Ky is beyond loyal. He is also crass; with a completely unfiltered mouth...I loved him instantly. He loves women and apologizes to no one regarding his promiscuous ways. It wasn’t until a certain Pilgrim blonde beauty emerged that things begin to change.I stared at the girl reflected in the glass, my lip curled in disgust. She was beautiful; flawless golden skin, long blonde hair, cornflower blue eyes...a most impressive disguise. The creation of utmost evil.Lilah is one of Mae’s sisters from the commune. All her life, she is lead to believe that she is a Cursed Woman of Eve. According to what she has been taught by her religion, her beauty is evil and lures men into temptation and into sin. Her beauty is a curse.After being rescued from the Order, all Lilah wants is to go back. She believes her salvation can only be obtained within the walls of the Order. She doesn’t know anything about living on the outside; her new surroundings scaring her. But what frightens her the most is Ky – the man who captivates her.No man can truly love a Cursed woman of Eve.And no woman of Eve will ever have the love of a pure soul.Because Lilah is Mae’s sister, and Styx will do anything to keep Mae happy, Styx orders Ky to teach and guide Lilah through her new surroundings. Lilah captivates Ky. For every smile she makes, he finds himself more enthralled by her. He finds satisfaction in teaching her and just flat out loves making her happy. She might be flat-out-crazy, especially with her bible-spewing-religious-loving-ways, but it doesn’t stop him from feeling protective of her. But can Lilah be convinced that Ky sees more in her than her beauty?“Every single second of my life...because it eventually led me to you...it lead me to fall so deeply in love with you, Ky...the man who recaptured this bruised and battered heart.”This story exceeded my expectations. Ky makes me smile just by being who he is. As for Lilah, she just broke my heart. Her life wasn’t easy and I kept up hope that she was going to find her happiness and freedom. After all the trials and obstacles these two went through, my heart is full knowing they found each other.This was another masterpiece by Tillie Cole and like many others out there, I know we’re all looking forward to the next book in this series. Bring on FLAME & MADDIE!!*Many thanks to Tillie for allowing me to Beta read this. It’s an honor ♥♥♥♥ FOLLOW ME ♥♥♥Blog:www.sweetspotbookblog.comFacebook:www.facebook.com/SweetSpotBookBlogTwitter:@thessamari

3.5 starsIn the beginning, this one was hard for me to really love. I didn't feel that strong bond between the couple like I did in book one and it was hard to love Ky after seeing him sleep around so much in the beginning of the book. And his choices in sleeping around actually ended putting Lilah in some pretty dangerous situations. This one is super dark and Lilah goes through a lot of really bad things. Ky wants to protect her and is definitely a "touch her and you die" hero. We have the same villains as book one and they're still trying to threaten the motorcycle club and destroy them. book.title: The Redemption of Callie & Kayden
book.author: Jessica Sorensen
book.description: From the New York Times bestselling author ofThe Coincidence of Callie & Kaydencomes an emotional new story about two troubled souls sharing one all-encompassing love . . .The dark secret Kayden has kept hidden for years is finally out. Worse, he's facing charges for battery. The only way to clear his name is for Callie to speak up-something he'll never ask her to do. Instead, he'll do whatever he must to protect her . . . even if it means letting go of the only girl he's ever loved.Callie knows Kayden is going back to his dark place and desperately wants to save him. But that means facing her greatest fear and admitting her own painful secrets aloud. The thought of breaking her silence terrifies her-but not as much as the thought of losing Kayden forever.Deep in her heart, Callie knows the time has come for her and Kayden to forget the pain of the past. With the help of her friends Seth and Luke she makes a plan to show Kayden the life they could have. But can she convince him they can make a fresh start together-or is she already too late?
