4.5"Why are you looking at me?""Because I like your face."In Leo, Mia Sheridan does it again. She brings romance, heart, grit, and tenderness to every story that she creates. I found myself swept away by this beautiful piece.Leo begins in the past. We meet Evie and Leo. Both wards of the state, they have found friendship, acceptance, and love in each other. Both having to endure the hardships of the foster system, they find solace in each other."Maybe I'm an open book, or maybe love is like a magnifying glass straight into the souls of those who own your heart."When Leo is fifteen, he is forced to say goodbye to Evie. He has been adopted and is moving away. He vows to find her when she is 18. They share a tender moment on the roof under a blanket of snow."I don't ever want to stop looking at him. I don't ever want to stop being near him. But he's moving across the country, and there's nothing we can do."-Evie8 years later....Evie is alone. She is making a way working as a maid. She is living a simple life. She enjoys reading and spending time with her friends. Only one thing is missing in her life....Leo!She recognizes a stranger following her."Someone is following me. He's been doing it for a week and a half now. He's crap at it. I marked him almost immediately and I've been watching him as he's been watching me. Clearly, he's no professional. But I can't think of one single reason why someone is following me around town."-EvieThe stalker turns out to be a friend of Leo's named Jake. Jake is everything Evie is not. He is a polished business man with plenty of money, polish, and class. He is drawn to her. She is drawn to him. Although Evie doesn't have much experience with men, she begins to spend time with Jake.Jake is a bit of a mystery. There are several instances where she fears he is lying to her. Evie is especially sensitive because of the numerous differences between the two. He is educated and running a multi million dollar company. Evie has her GED and is working as a maid. Every time Evie hesitates with Jake, he draws her back in."Please, just trust me, please don't go."-Jake"I follow Jake and my heart once again. But hoping deep inside, that that doesn’t make me a fool."-EvieTheir relationship soon transforms into a passionate love affair. Jake and Evie find connection on every level. This is a tender friends to lovers story."He takes the back of my head in his hand and tilts it so that he can plunge his tongue in deep, and then we're kissing like we can't get enough of each other, like if a herd of zebras trampled through his living room right now, we wouldn't even come up for air."-EvieOverall, Leo was a beautiful read. All of the quesitons are answered. I won't lie, I private messaged a friend with several questions along the way. In true Sheridan fashion, she has crafted a story that will stay with me forever. I can recommend with out hesitancy this read to anyone that enjoys a tender romance and a story of broken things made beautiful. There was a high level of angst as you try to put the pieces together. Still, it was an unforgettable read! Days later I am still thinking about it.

3 StarsThis sweet and touching story greatly showcases the power of love and the unsurpassable beauty ofredemption. It opens with an emotional glimpse into the childhood of these broken characters, and quickly unites with present time-- sprinkling in important flashbacks as the story progresses.Eviehas come a long way from the lost and neglected little girl she once was. But one thing remains constant--her undying longing for her childhood best friend and first love--Leo. Although she has accepted his disappearance and his broken promises, she continues to quietly save her heart for the boy who broke it all those years ago...I'm going to kiss you now, Evie, and when I do, it's going to mean that you're mine.WhenJakestalks into Evie's life, armed with determination and information on Leo, Evie's world seems to finally come alive again. He sparks things inside her that she's never felt, but he also harbors secrets that could ultimately break the solid ground she now stands upon.I really enjoyed the way this story progressed, and how we were gradually introduced to its secrets. The twist in the plot was easy to decipher, but I got the feeling that we werepurposelybeing clued in ahead of time. As if the shock value of this story was meant for the characters and not necessarily the readers—allowing us the suspense of watching the truths unfold.Jake was a passionate, bossy, and sexy hero. There were some areas of his character that lacked a little depth, but his guarded persona was warranted. The steamy scenes intense and descriptively sensual.Evie—hands down—MADE this story, for me! Heractionsbuilt her character, not her inner dialogue, which was a true testament to this author's amazing execution. Instead of wallowing in thewhy me's, Evie uses her tortured childhood as motivation to rise above. The world has given her so many reasons to never trust it again--but she does. Heartache, neglect, poverty, and instability has plagued the precious years of her youth-- but sheperseveres. Not only is she an inspirational heroine, but an amazing example of how every negative in life can be used to gain a positive perspective. Evie, and all that she represents, became the heart and soul of this book.Very sweet read!"Why are you looking at me?""Because I like your face."Book Stats:▪	 Genre/Category:Romance/NA▪	 Steam Caliber:Extremely steamy▪ Romance:Slow burn, intense, adorable.▪	 Characters:Broken and garded. Sexy, bossy hero. Lovable heroine.▪	 Plot:Unique execution. Twist in plot.▪ Writing:Engaging.▪ POV:1st Person: Heroine▪	 Cliffhanger:None▪	 Next Installment:POV switch

This is a pretty good story of friends to lovers, second chance kind of love between two children who met in foster care. One gets adopted, one gets left behind but they promise to find each other when they are eighteen. ~Sigh~ Of course,thatdoesn't happen.I have a lot of conflicting feelings about this story. I was intrigued and couldn't put the book down but it was blatantly obvious who a certain person was. Fortunately, there are enough twists that it keeps you on your toes and keeps you interested...well, at least it did for me.Both Leo and Evie are quite enjoyable and you just can't help but pull for them especially with all they've been through. And they're just so sweet in that first puppy love kind of way.Fast forward eight years and Evie has someone following her. Turns out it's a friend of Leo's named Jake who was sent to check in on Evie. Things progress quite quickly but there are things about Jake that just don't add up and slowly but surely, things come to light.Jake and Evie were quite a lovely couple as well but their relationship was also a bit disconcerting. What Jake did was not right but Evie should've taken timethento figure out things and really look at the signs.There's drama and there's angst but finally we get some answers and it's as bad as we thought it would be. Luckily, Evie has a great support system and is able to get to her true feelings.I thought things got resolved a bit too quickly but overall I was happy with the story and the epilogue was really sweet.There's a second book from Leo's POV calledLeo's Chance. Hopefully, it's not a total rehash of the same story and we get some good insights.(ETA: It is a rehash but there are plenty of new things to keep things moving along. But more than that...the ending and extra epilogues make it highly worth reading!)Favorite quote:♥ "I can remain steady and strong, because I believe in you. Because you are mine."

4.5 stars{Everyone tells a story about themselves in their own head. That story makes you what you are, dictating all your actions and all your mistakes. If your own story is filled with guilt and fear and self-hatred, life can look pretty miserable.But, if you're very lucky, you might have a person who tells you a better story, one that takes up residence in your soul, speaking louder than the woeful tale you've convinced yourself of. If you let it speak loudly within your heart, it becomes your passion and your purpose. And this is a good thing, the best of things. Because it is the very definition of love, nothing less.}

Leo is the debut novel by one of my favourite authors, Mia Sheridan. I've read a few that she has written after this one, which I have really liked, but this one...let me just add that I am in the minority here...didn't work for me. It failed to excite me.......I'm going to try and keep my "rant" as short as possible.The writing was nothing like the other books...it was just okay. No great quotes stood out to me!!!There were so many things that glaringly reminded me of 50 Shades of Grey.....all that internal dialogue, the broken and dominant hero, the sweet and virginal heroine, etc, etcSo, the heroine is still at a virgin....she promised to wait for Leo, remember.......and then..........presto.............2 dates and she sleeps with the hero...who is not Leo!!!!Can I do some eye exercises here please!!!!!I actually liked Evie, the heroine. She was a really sweet and likeable characters......but when she starts telling her besties and friends all of Leo's secrets and how much she loves him....blah blah..........I just couldn't believable that any sane person who do that, even if it's her very best best BFF...............I couldn't engage with Jake, the hero.........his whole attitude was ...ooh I'm so great.........and quite honestly, I can't believe a guy that age would be running a company like he does!!! Oh, did you say he's like Mr Christian Grey????? Really!!! Guess there's a lot of hot hot very very rich very very young zillionaires out there that I am not aware of!!!Or not!!! UmmmYes, some more eye exercises after this......Evie sleeps with Jake....lots and lots and lots of times and she never never ever sees the ginormous tattoo on his back.........what, they just do the missionary position in the dark!!!! Seriously!!! And to test my intelligence further....Evie DOES NOT recognize Leo when she sees him after all those many years........come one...so, he's had plastic surgery but still, I found this part very hard to swallow.......So when Evie discovers Jake's big secret - she's heartbroken for him...naturally!!! She's a sweet and loving and caring person.......and she's totally so understanding......really!!! The guy has slept his way through the years.....she's remained a virgin.........allright, so I can't exactly expect a person to keep their virginity in this day and age......but why can he be a slut and she has to be a virgin!!!!Jake's sob story ..............I was like......Despite the disappointment, I am still going ahead and reading the remainder of MS books........and I can't wait.As they say, you can't win them all..............

I really wanted to love this book. However, despite its promising premise, I was disappointed.Unfortunately, this book suffered from far too many editing problems. Most of the dialogue tags were punctuated incorrectly, with periods at the end of the dialogue where commas should’ve been used instead. Furthermore, you can’t speak an action. A period is needed as a separator between the action and the dialogue, not a comma.I'm sorry, but I’m of the opinion that if you’re going to put your book out on the market for people to buy, then it should be properly edited.The writing was boring, not the mention awkward at times. Every time the two main characters spoke to each other, whether in person or on the phone, they constantly said the other person’s name. Actually, this problem wasn’t limited to just the main characters. People don’t talk like that.The story was rife with telling instead of showing. But, not to worry, there were plenty of smutty scenes to make up for it.Sorry, but an overabundance of smut is not a substitute for lack of plot or character development. Evie would start her day and proceed totellus everything by using a heavy dose of info dumping.Both Evie and Jake were one dimensional characters. Jake was basically a watered-down version of Christian Grey, minus the whole BDSM aspect. I mean, seriously, this book even featured a female pedophile.Evie fell in love with Jake after just knowing him a few days. Let's not forget that they met because he was stalking her. With Evie’s background, which included molestation, I found this a little hard to believe. I guess having 'chemistry' with a perfect stranger overrules common sense. Who cares about personal safety when the person stalking you is Adonis personified? She was a little too perfect, far too nice, and of course, extremely beautiful, but totally unaware of said beauty. No one let her forget these things either. People constantly praised her spectacular beauty and good-doer nature. It's like, okay, I get it. You don't have to ram it down my throat every chapter. I didn't forget. If her Mary-Sue tendencies were toned down, she could’ve been a more enjoyable character.I wish the plot had been meatier and less predictable. I have to admit that, at times, I felt like I was reading an imitation Kristen Ashley book, slight though it may be. It was just little things here and there. Anyway, take that for what you will. All in all, though, it wasn't a bad read. Not great, but not terrible, either.

If only I read Leo 4 years ago, I might give it 4 stars or even 5 stars. However my taste for romance had been changed. And I am sorry to say I am disappointed.I can not believe Leo is written by Miss Sheridan. The author who give Archer's Voice. So many problem is just.... wrong. The chemistry is rushed and forced. Their banter is off. Nothing interesting on the plot. The jokes are bland.I was having difficult time finishing this book. And the climax about who is Jake, well I already seen it since the first chapter.2 stars

Rating Clarification 3.5 StarsI have to be honest and say that when I first started this book I really didn't like it. Once i got into a little further I started to like it. There are twists in this book however I felt like I knew them all along. It wasn't a huge surprise. It was a good book overall. Just not one I would say to run out and read as soon as possible.

4.5 StarsI look into his eyes and ask, “Why are you looking at me?”His eyes search mine for several long seconds before he hold contact and whispers, “Because I like your face.”ATran recommended this book to me yesterday and after I read the blurb, I knew I had to read it ASAP. I luckily found it on Smashwords a day early than its scheduled release date and just dove right in. I was intrigued immediately. The story began with our H/h, Leo and Evie, only 15 and 14 respectively. The setting was on a rooftop, snow slowly starting to come down, not wanting to say goodbye to each other. Both children of the foster care system, Leo has been adopted and is moving. He promises to write her and come back for her when she turns eighteen so that they can begin their life together.“I’m going to kiss you now, Evie, and when I do, it’s going to mean that you’re mine. I don’t care how far away from each other we are. You. Are. Mine. I’ll wait for you. And I want you to wait for me. Promise me you won’t let anyone else touch you. Promise me you’ll save yourself for me.”Oh yea. I was SOLD.Fast forward eight years…Evie has not heard a word from Leo. The last time she saw or heard from him was atop the roof eight years ago. She is now twenty-two and has worked hard to get where she is. She’s not rich by any means, works two jobs to pay rent, but no matter what, she is happy with her life. Knowing where she has been, she doesn’t take anything for granted. Her life is uneventful for the most part, except for the stalker that has been following her for last week and half. He doesn’t give off creepy vibes, especially since he looks harmless, and gorgeous, too. She’s actually amused at how obvious he is. She decides to confront him and find out why he’s stalking her.Jake is there to fulfill Leo’s dying wish: to check on Evie and make sure she’s okay. This is how Evie finds out what has happened to her Leo: A car accident claimed his life. She doesn’t really know how to process this information, nor does she understand why Jake keeps showing up after confronting him. But it’s pretty obvious that they have an immediate connection. Evie’s beauty and, most of all, her kindness, draws him in.The more Evie and Jake spend with each other, the more Evie falls madly for him, but she remains hesitant. Jake is holding something back. We know barely anything about him and on a few occasions, makes Evie doubt his level of honesty. What do really know about Jake? What is he hiding?Totally bossy. But totally sweet. And totally hot.“Evie,” he says quietly, finally looking at me, “what I mean is, when I fuck you, you’re going to be mine. Is that clear enough for you?”I honestly couldn’t put the book down. Not only was I enjoying Jake’s bossiness and aggressiveness, but most of all I wanted to know exactly who he is. What was he not telling us? Throughout the book, I was trying to read between the lines, so to speak. I formed a theory, but nothing was confirmed until towards the end. By then, I was completely engrossed wanting to know the connection. There were times where my heart actually broke for both Evie and Jake. This is definitely an emotional story and would recommend it. But be warned, the story could’ve used a little editing, but not to the point of annoyance really, but I thought I’d just mention it anyway. Happy reading!
2.75 Stars
